description: Empty template — fill in id, turns, gold_documents (doc_id values from data/kb/metadata/documents.csv), and optional fields. Remove unused optional arrays or leave them empty.

turns: Summarize the key ideas of lecture 3 in high dimensional data week 3.
rubric_hints: Answer must mention at least one of the following: scaling laws, Self-Supervised Learning, or DINO.

turns: When is assessment 1 of generative AI due?
rubric_hints: Answer must clearly state the due date is 24/04/2026 3:00 pm

turns: Give me tips for habit formation based on all the habit formation research papers in the external knowledge KB
rubric_hints: Answer should mention at least one of the following tips: to start with small, simple actions, link the habit to a consistent cue, practice in the morning, select habits you personally value, be realistic about the timeline, focus on consistency and not perfection, use planning and monitoring tools, or focus on adding not subtracting.

turns: Suggest a study environment to work on a group project. I want a laid-back atmosphere, with sofas and natural light. Justify your recommendation.
rubric_hints: Answer should mention env_07 (common study lounge) and argue that it's a good fit because it has sofas and large windows that let in natural light.

turns: Is the common room a good place to do administrative tasks if my priority is comfortable seating?
rubric_hints: Agent should answer no, explicitly stating that the common room has uncomfortable seatings/stools.

turns: In lecture 4 of hdd week 4, briefly explain the key ideas of the diagram in page 3 of the PDF
rubric_hints: Answer should explain that DINO trains a student network to match the outputs of a teacher network in a self-supervised way.
The teacher should be described as providing stable targets, often via a moving-average update.
The response should mention that the framework learns meaningful representations while avoiding collapse/trivial solutions.
The answer should mention at least one of the following details: prototypes, stop-gradient, and mimicking a large batch size.

turns: Estimate the duration to complete assignment 1 of generative AI and justify your answer. Then, suggest a good indoor study environment to work on the assignment.
rubric_hints: Duration should be around 6-10 hours. The justification should mention that the assingment involves coding and report writing. The study environment should be the private study room (env_03) or the common study space (env_06).

turns: Estimate the time to memorize all of lecture 4 of hdd week 4. Then, find a free slot in my schedule on Wednesday of that week to do that.
rubric_hints: Duration should be around 4-6 hours. A free slot is on Wednesday 18th March from 10:45 to 15:30.

turns: State the key modeling variables on the applied class example of operations research week 6. Then, estimate the time to review that example.
rubric_hints: Duration should be around 1-2 hours. Answer should mention at least one of the following variables: Xtp units of product p to make in month t; Stp units of product p to store at the end of month t; Ytp, units of product p to sell in month t.

turns: What are the main topics for high dimensional data tutorial week 5.
How long would it take me to revise that material seriously?
rubric_hints: Answer should mention at least one of the following topics: Multimodal Large Language Models in the context of RAG, the three strategies for multimodal RAG, or basic code to implement MRAG. Duration should be around 1-2 hours.

turns: Find free slots for a 2-hour study session on Wednesday of week 3.
Same thing but for Thursday instead.
rubric_hints: Wednesday 11th March from 11:00 to 13:00 or from 13:30 to 15:30. Thursday 12th March from 13:30 to 17:30 and 18:30 to 21:00.

turns: Summarize generative AI week 2 lecture topics.
Where's a good outdoor place to revise that material if I want a proper desk and why?
rubric_hints: Answer should mention at least one of the following topics: Foundations of Large Language Modeling, Attention, Transformers, or Text Generation. It should recommend the outdoor courtyard (env_01), explicitly stating that it's the better option because the desk is more suitable for studying than that of the outdoor lawn (env_02).
